ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANT KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC.'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED IN PART, SUSTAINED IN PART AND OFF CALENDAR IN PART. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER AS TO THE FIFTH (NEGLIGENCE-WRONGFUL DEATH) AND SIXTH (STRICT LIABILITY-WRONGFUL DEATH) CAUSES OF ACTION IS OVERRULED BECAUSE THOSE CAUSES OF ACTION DO NOT INVOLVE THE SAME PRIMARY RIGHT AS THE CAUSES OF ACTION FROM THE PERSONAL INJURY ACTION AND THUS CCP 430.10(C) IS INAPPLICABLE. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION (FALSE REPRESENTATION) IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND PURSUANT TO CCP 430.10(C). DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE FIRST (NEGLIGENCE-SURVIVAL) AND SECOND (STRICT LIABILITY-SURVIVAL) CAUSES OF ACTION AND PLAINTIFFS' CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES IS OFF CALENDAR BECAUSE PLAINTIFFS HAVE DISMISSED THEM. ONE ORDER WAS SIGNED FOR BOTH MOTIONS. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, MATTER NOT REPORTED

ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANT KAISER GYPSUM CO., INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT IS GRANTED IN PART AND OFF CALENDAR IN PART. DEFENDANT'S MOTION IS GRANTED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO ANY DAMAGES ALLEGED PURSUANT TO CCP 377.34, AS THOSE ARE NOT ALLOWED IN A WRONGFUL DEATH ACTION. (CCP 377.61.) DEFENDANT'S MOTION IS OFF CALENDAR AS TO PUNITIVE DAMAGES AS PLAINTIFFS HAVE DISMISSED THAT CLAIM. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE THE PRAYER FOR DAMAGES BASED ON LOSS OF CONSORTIUM IN THE WRONGFUL DEATH CASE ONLY, IS GRANTED. ONE ORDER FOR BOTH MOTIONS WAS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: TERI L. Off Calendar Apr-03-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Erica J. Saxum, Esq., of DEHAY ELLISTON LLP (510-285-0750), appearing for defendant / moving party Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc
Gilbert L. Purcell, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Patricia Casey, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT

No argument was made and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.'s motion to strike portions of plaintiffs' second amended complaint: As to prayer for damage (a), brought by defendant only as to Patricia Casey, defendant's motion is denied. Patricia Casey is not barred from making a claim for general damages according to proof and applicable law at trial. As to prayers for damage (b), (c), (d), (e), (g) and (h), defendant's motion is denied as moot as to Patricia Casey because she has dismissed those claims against defendant and defendant's motion is granted without leave to amend as to Catherine Grabinski and Jessica Casey. Catherine Grabinski and Jessica Casey have not dismissed any of their claims for damages against defendant. As wrongful death plaintiffs, they are barred from making a claim for these damages and thus leave to amend is denied because they cannot cure such defects. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez, CSR #12436 (503/TLJ)

RULING:
DEFENDANT KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.'s demurrer to plaintiffs' complaint is overruled in part, sustained in part and off calendar in part. Defendant's demurrer as to the fifth (negligence-wrongful death) and sixth (strict liability-wrongful death) causes of action is overruled because those causes of action do not involve the same primary right as the causes of action from the personal injury action and thus CCP 430.10(c) is inapplicable. Defendant's demurrer as to the third cause of action (false representation) is sustained with 10 days leave to amend pursuant to CCP 430.10(c). Defendant's demurrer to the first (negligence-survival) and second (strict liability-survival) causes of action and plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages is off calendar because plaintiffs have dismissed them. One order was signed for both motions. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported = (503/TLJ)
